subject: Add Fanatec wheel driver (#24)
author: Michael Huang

impact.effect_if_absent: Fanatec racing wheels (ClubSport V2/V25, CSL Elite, CSR Elite, Podium DD1/DD2, CSL DD) would not be recognized or controllable as HID input devices; force feedback, pedal calibration, LED control, and steering range adjustment would be unavailable

userspace_coupling.interface: vendor_id 0x0eb7 device enumeration; sysfs attributes for range, load, rumble, display, tuning, LEDs; input event codes with BTN_WHEEL bit set for pedal recognition
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5006 (Fanatec Wheels vendor ID 0x0eb7 check and device-specific handling); 5049 (set_wheel_range call with cfg.wheel_range parameter)

forward_port.effort: carried with modifications: hrtimer_init() + assignment pattern replaced with hrtimer_setup(); missing-prototypes warnings fixed via static keywords and extern declarations in hid-ftec.h; unused variable cleanup

license_provenance.author: gotzl (upstream project author)
license_provenance.origin_project_status: never merged into mainline Linux; remains out-of-tree

notes: This commit introduces a complete new HID driver for Fanatec racing wheels ported from the gotzl/hid-fanatecff out-of-tree project. The driver has two components: hid-ftec.c (probe, sysfs interfaces for load/rumble/display, LED management via led_classdev) and hid-ftecff.c (force-feedback implementation via hrtimer-based effect scheduler). Critical feature: pedal calibration via sysfs 'load' attribute and steering range control via 'range' attribute are essential for hardware compatibility. The driver is tightly coupled to Main_MiSTer userspace via vendor_id checks and wheel-range configuration (verified: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5006 `input[i].vid == 0x0eb7` and :5049 `set_wheel_range(i, cfg.wheel_range)`). Verified not present in vanilla Linux 6.18.38 (no hid-ftec.c, hid-ftecff.c, or HID_FTEC configuration option). Carried patch 0012-hid-fanatec.patch already includes forward-port adjustments for 6.18 (hrtimer_init()+manual ->function assign -> hrtimer_setup(), confirmed present in vanilla include/linux/hrtimer.h:230; -Wmissing-prototypes fixes, confirmed default-on via /mnt/source/linux/scripts/Makefile.extrawarn:22; unused variable cleanup). All 10 device_ids re-derived against drivers/hid/hid-ftec.h in the fork commit (FANATEC_VENDOR_ID 0x0eb7 + 10 product IDs) and confirmed against the devices[] hid_device_id table in hid-ftec.c; every affected_hardware name maps to its correct ID. Disposition in patch-provenance.md already correctly classified as Class D carry (line 360). Author is PR contributor (#24, #25 Michael Huang, PR #24 also includes fix commit #25 folded in). No upstream equivalence exists. SONNET-AUDIT FLAG (not a record error, flagging for patch maintainer): the 0012-hid-fanatec.patch header comment for hid-ftecff.c contains a stray line — 'Decode fixes from pcnimdock (https://github.com/pcnimdock/guncon3) -- unrelated project note kept for provenance parity with upstream source comment' — that is copy-paste contamination from the unrelated GunCon3 driver's provenance note (pcnimdock authored GunCon3 decode fixes, not anything Fanatec-related); the patch file itself should be corrected even though this record's license_provenance field does not repeat the error.
sonnet-verified: amended — device_ids format fixed (stripped '0x' prefixes, normalized case, to match VVVV:PPPP schema convention). All 10 device IDs and their hardware-name mapping re-derived and confirmed against the fork's drivers/hid/hid-ftec.h FANATEC_VENDOR_ID/#define block and the devices[] hid_device_id table in hid-ftec.c — every affected_hardware entry maps to the correct ID. hrtimer_setup() and -Wmissing-prototypes forward-port claims confirmed against live 6.18 checkout. Main_MiSTer input.cpp:5006/5049 citations verified verbatim. Flagged (not amended, out of scope for this record's authority): 0012-hid-fanatec.patch's hid-ftecff.c header comment contains a stray 'pcnimdock/guncon3' provenance note that is copy-paste contamination from the unrelated GunCon3 patch — patch file needs a maintainer fix, noted in this record's notes field.
